description: This figure presents comprehensive microstructural characterization of Zr-alloyed Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels with varying zirconium contents (Zr0, Zr0.01, and Zr0.50 wt.%) following identical thermo-mechanical processing. EBSD inverse pole figure (IPF) maps reveal distinct grain structure evolution from homogeneous fully recrystallized equiaxed FCC grains in Zr0 (average size ~12 μm) to heterogeneous bimodal distributions in Zr0.50 comprising coarse grain zones (~5 μm, 65% area fraction) and fine grain zones (~1 μm, 35% area fraction). Corresponding kernel average misorientation (KAM) maps indicate high geometrically necessary dislocation densities localized within fine grain regions. Backscattered electron imaging confirms uniform distribution of nanoscale ZrC precipitates (~30 nm) preferentially situated in fine grain areas. The figure illustrates that Zr addition induces solute drag and Zener pinning effects that retard recrystallization, creating high-angle hetero-interfaces. This heterogeneous grain structure promotes strain partitioning between soft coarse grains and hard fine grains, enabling superior strength-ductility synergy evidenced by yield strength increasing from 435 MPa (Zr0) to 505 MPa (Zr0.50) while simultaneously improving elongation from 52% to 58%.
